capability_objective: Reconcile two records: search for Pool sedan 7 in Depot A and Accessible van 4 in North Garage together, then retrieve the unique matches together. If Accessible van 4 is still maintenance-due, change only that record to maintenance-scheduled; otherwise make no change. Leave Pool sedan 7 and every similarly named record untouched. Do not touch similarly named, related, or archived records. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile two fleet records in this sandbox through the genuine local `./fleet-records` executable, invoked only with Pi's Bash tool. Begin by reading the executable's built-in help. Treat the executable as the sole interface to fleet records. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing or operation-history files, or anything under `.harness`; do not manufacture operation evidence or change fleet state by writing files directly.

First search for the exact vehicle name `Pool sedan 7` in `Depot A` and the exact vehicle name `Accessible van 4` in `North Garage`. Start these independent searches as two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one fleet-record operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, launch background jobs, broaden either search, or begin a retrieval until both searches have finished. Continue only if each search returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID. If either lookup is unresolved or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval and write, identify the affected lookup, and do not guess.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve both complete records by their respective returned IDs. Start the two retrievals as two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes the fleet executable, again with exactly one fleet-record operation in each call. Do not use an ID from any other source. Wait for both retrievals before evaluating the condition. If either complete record is missing, mismatches its search, or has no status, stop without changing fleet state.

Evaluate only the status returned by the complete `Accessible van 4` record. If and only if that retrieved status is exactly `maintenance-due`, make one later fleet-record operation that changes only that returned record to `maintenance-scheduled`; otherwise make no change. Never update `Pool sedan 7`. Leave every same-name, similarly named, related, archived, and inactive record untouched. Do not create, archive, cancel, delete, or notify anything; do not issue any other update or registry operation.

After the conditional branch, run `python3 .harness/verify.py`. Report the two returned IDs, names, locations, and retrieved statuses, then say whether the conditional update occurred and give its returned final status if it did. Base the report only on executable output and the verifier result; do not imply a skipped or failed operation succeeded.
